mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment rise, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series are above their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL levels above 300 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2% signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates above 2% consistently precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the predictive power remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction and impending recession, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in > 60% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines, or the signal loses predictive significance during late-cycle regimes.
